findings.reachability: Any authenticated WordPress user who can supply a valid woocommerce-settings nonce. In practice that nonce is only rendered on pages requiring manage_woocommerce, but the handler itself contains no capability check — the authorization is entirely implicit and relies on external nonce availability.
findings.justification: block_save_settings() fires on admin_init, verifies wp_verify_nonce(..., 'woocommerce-settings') for CSRF protection, but never calls current_user_can() before executing wp_die() to block a settings save; the nonce is not a substitute for authorization.

detection_pattern.section_name: Broken Access Control: admin_init handler without capability check
detection_pattern.description: Finds admin_init action handlers. Files matching this pattern but lacking current_user_can() calls in the registered callback may perform privileged actions without proper authorization. A nonce alone is CSRF protection, not authorization.
